mission.title: Install and Configure K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
mission.description: K3s is a lightweight Kubernetes distribution designed for resource-constrained environments. It is ideal for edge computing, IoT, and development scenarios where a full Kubernetes installation may be overkill. K3s simplifies the installation process by bundling components into a single binary, but it may not support all Kubernetes features.

steps.title: Install K3s
steps.description:
This command installs K3s as a single binary on your system, which is lightweight and easy to manage.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

```

> This installs the latest version of K3s.

steps.title: Verify K3s Installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the K3s service to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

sudo systemctl status k3s

```

> Look for 'active (running)' in the output.

steps.title: Set Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Copy the Kubeconfig file to your home directory for easy access to the K3s cluster.

```bash

sudo cp /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml ~/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) ~/.kube/config

```

> This allows you to use kubectl with your K3s cluster.
> Ensure the config file is owned by your user.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the K3s node is ready and available in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see your node listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features like metrics server and RBAC for better management.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml

kubectl create clusterrolebinding default-admin --clusterrole=cluster-admin --serviceaccount=kube-system:kube-admin

```

> This installs the metrics server.
> This grants admin privileges to the default service account.

uninstall.title: Uninstall K3s
uninstall.description:
This command will remove K3s and all associated components. Be aware that this will delete all workloads and data in the cluster.

```bash

sudo /usr/local/bin/k3s-uninstall.sh

```

> This script cleans up all K3s resources.

uninstall.title: Remove Kubeconfig
uninstall.description:
Delete the kubeconfig file to prevent access to the now-uninstalled cluster.

```bash

rm -f ~/.kube/config

```

> This removes the kubeconfig file from your home directory.

upgrade.title: Upgrade K3s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster data. After upgrading, verify that all components are functioning correctly.

```bash

sudo cp /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml ~/.kube/config.bak

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup your current kubeconfig.
> This will upgrade K3s to the latest version.
> Verify that the nodes are still in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k3s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The K3s service may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service status with 'sudo systemctl status k3s' and restart it if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be correctly set up or permissions may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubeconfig file is copied correctly and has the right permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns no nodes
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** K3s may not be running or the kubeconfig is not pointing to the right cluster.

**Fix:**
Verify K3s is running and check your kubeconfig file for the correct server address.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The container image may not be available or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and verify the image name and tag.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for ARM64 architecture, improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older storage backends like etcd.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to migrate any etcd data to sqlite3.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' with your node listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the resource requirements for running K3s.
